key_insights.insight: Reframing anxiety as excitement can improve performance by focusing on opportunities instead of threats.
key_insights.quote: The reframing anxiety as excitement paper is lots of different ways to convince yourself that you're feeling excited just by saying I'm excited out loud. And in doing so, that makes you focus on opportunities rather than threats, how things could go well rather than poorly.

key_insights.insight: The most effective part of an apology is not dwelling on excuses but expressing remorse and promising concrete changes in behavior.
key_insights.quote: What is more effective is what you're describing, taking ownership and saying, look, I just messed up and I'm so sorry and I feel awful about it. And the most effective component of an apology is actually making a promise to change.

key_insights.insight: Instead of immediately disagreeing, validating the other person's feelings makes them feel heard and can lead to more productive conversations.
key_insights.quote: It makes sense that you feel X about Y. Whatever people are feeling, whatever they express to you, we can validate that feeling because whatever's going on in their mind is their reality.

key_insights.insight: To keep conversations from becoming boring, one must move past small talk, prep topics ahead of time, and ask more personalized follow-up questions.
key_insights.quote: The mistake that people make is they stay there too long, way too long, any more than like one beat of, oh my goodness, the weather's really warm. It's like summer in California. Then you need to make it more personal and move up this topic pyramid.

key_insights.insight: A key indicator for friendship is vulnerability, by sharing struggles, hopes, and dreams; Men tend to avoid this more often.
key_insights.quote: It was so hard to watch is that they don't, they really struggled with vulnerability. Vulnerability is such a key component of friendship...It was so maddening to watch these men have hundreds of conversations and like none of them asked those questions or talked about those things with each other.

key_insights.insight: One can signal their interest to others by asking questions and that it makes others feel heard.
key_insights.quote: It makes them feel heard and like you want to know their answer that you're interested in them. So it signals your interest, but also you learn what's in their mind and what their experience is, which arms you with more information to then ask more better questions.

key_insights.insight: Those who are on the higher end of the corporate ladder recognize that their conversation skills got them there and work towards improving them.
key_insights.quote: In fact, the higher and more successful people are, the more likely they are to be aware that this is really important and that they have room for improvement. It's almost like you're aware that this skill is probably what helped you get where you are, and therefore you want to get even better at it.

key_insights.insight: When someone asks a question and you only bring it back to yourself or what you know/experienced you are "boomer asking", which detracts from the original point.
key_insights.quote: So I say to you, like,Stephen, what's your favorite restaurant?Mr. Chow's. Oh, I've been to Mr. Chow's.Last time I went to Mr. Chow's, I went with a whole bunch of friends, and I had a friend who was really...People do that all the time.

actionable_advice.advice: Before high-stakes conversations, tell yourself you're excited to focus on the potential upsides.
actionable_advice.how_to: Repeat phrases like 'I'm excited' to change your appraisal of the situation and focus on opportunities rather than threats.

actionable_advice.advice: If you have wronged someone during conversation, apologize effectively with taking accountability and making a promise to do better next time.
actionable_advice.how_to: Acknowledge you messed up, don't revisit excuses, and follow through on a plan that will make you never make that mistake again.

actionable_advice.advice: In order to persuade, actively validate someone by saying "it makes sense that you feel X about Y"
actionable_advice.how_to: If someone is arguing a completely contrarian belief, begin by saying "it makes sense that you feel that way" to allow them to feel open, heard, and safe to voice out their opinion.

actionable_advice.advice: When communicating on the internet, use tactics such as intentional misspelling to seem more human.
actionable_advice.how_to: In a world where Artificial Intelligence is the current and inevitable future, try to use unique and real text like 'lol' to remind someone they are talking to you. Disregard the constant need to be grammatically correct.

actionable_advice.advice: Show kindness to others by prepping topics ahead of time to demonstrate how valuable they are to you.
actionable_advice.how_to: 30 seconds before calling someone, think of what they would be excited to talk about to be thoughtful and give them space to talk without you interrupting to go off-topic.

memorable_stories: The guest talks about a time her research on reframing anxiety as excitement was featured in the movie 'Inside Out'
The host discusses a time when he interviewed someone with a colleague and had to direct the other person in the room to not veer off topic.
The host discusses an acquaintance who only spoke "I disagree" and how it made him back off.
The guest discusses how she had her students do office hours in person with her and through an AI bot. She was surprised at the results.
contrarian_claims: Contrary to common advice, one can over-apologize and harm a conversation.
Being interesting can be less valuable than being interested. A goal of conversation is to learn about that person rather than telling them a ton about you.
Listening isn't just nodding; one should verbally confirm that you are listening by asking follow-up questions. Also, active listening is normative and not necessarily genuine.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Harvard’s Behaviour Expert: The Psychology Of Why People Don't Like You!
